articles.title: Trump says war on Iran will be 'over quickly'
articles.summary: Trump says war on Iran will be 'over quickly' US President Donald Trump said that the war on Iran will be "over quickly", as reports indicate Washington and Tehran moving closer to a memorandum. "When you look at the kind of things that are happening, we are doing that for one very important reason: We cannot allow them to have a nuclear weapon," Trump said during a tele-rally for Georgia Republican governor candidate Burt Jones. "So I think most people understand that. They understand that what we are doing is right, and it'll be over quickly," he added.

articles.title: US, Iran Weigh Potential Deal, Trump Says ‘We Won’
articles.summary: Trump told reporters at the White House that he didn’t have a deadline to reach an agreement with Iran, but predicted “it’ll happen” and declared “we won” the war. Meanwhile, the US and Iran are circling around a fresh proposal. Bloomberg’s Jeff Mason reports.

articles.title: Israeli assault across Lebanon kills 16 people, wounds over a dozen more
articles.summary: Israeli assault across Lebanon kills 16 people, wounds over a dozen more Israeli drone and air strikes in southern and eastern Lebanon have killed 16 people, Lebanese officials said on Wednesday. At least 21 others, including paramedics, have been wounded in these attacks. Amongst the areas hit were a school, a number of residential areas and civilian infrastructure.  Since March 2, Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed over 2,710 people and wounded more than 8,35o others. articles.summary: Hamas calls on US, mediators to stop the escalating Israeli attacks in Gaza In a press statement, Hamas said the latest Israeli attacks waged on Gaza represent a "blatant violation" of the ceasefire agreement signed in October.  "The US administration and the guarantor states of the Sharm El-Sheikh agreement must take immediate action to restrain the fascist occupation and compel it to cease its aggression against innocent civilians in the Gaza Strip," the movement added.  On Wednesday, several civilians were killed and wounded in Israeli air strikes across the besieged enclave.

Eight military vehicles reportedly advanced toward a bridge in Wadi al-Raqad, located in the Yarmouk Basin, and subsequently deployed across the area.  The latest incursion comes less than a week before Israeli forces carried out a similar raid into the nearby town of Jamlah. articles.title: Hezbollah says it carried out 17 attacks on Israeli forces in Lebanon
articles.summary: Hezbollah says it carried out 17 attacks on Israeli forces in Lebanon Hezbollah said its fighters launched 17 attacks on Israeli forces operating inside Lebanese territory on Wednesday. In a statement, the group said it targeted Israeli soldiers and military vehicles in Qantara, Biyyada, Taybeh, Naqoura, Rishaf and Aita al-Shaab using drones and rockets. Hezbollah also said it struck Israeli military equipment, including a personnel carrier in Qaouzah and a newly established command centre in Qantara.

articles.title: Dollar weakens as investors bet on end to Iran war
articles.summary: Dollar weakens as investors bet on end to Iran war The US dollar fell against most major currencies on Wednesday as investors reacted positively to signs the US-Israeli war on Iran could move towards a resolution. The dollar index, which tracks the currency against six major peers, dropped 0.4 percent to 97.941 after earlier falling to 97.623. The index reached its lowest level since late February, before Israeli and US strikes on Iran triggered the current conflict. The euro rose 0.6 percent to $1.1755, while the British pound gained 0.6 percent to $1.36125.

articles.title: Iran says ports ready to support shipping in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iran says ports ready to support shipping in Strait of Hormuz Iran’s Ports and Maritime Organisation said the country’s ports are “fully ready” to provide maritime services, technical assistance and medical support to commercial vessels travelling through the Strait of Hormuz and surrounding regional waters. The statement, carried by Iran’s state news agency IRNA, came after US President Donald Trump paused the US military’s “Project Freedom” operation in the strait, citing progress in negotiations with Iran over the US-Israeli war on Iran.

articles.title: Oil prices fall as markets react to possible Hormuz agreement
articles.summary: Oil prices fall as markets react to possible Hormuz agreement Oil prices dropped sharply on Wednesday while stock markets rose globally amid hopes that the US and Iran may be moving towards an agreement that would allow shipping through the Strait of Hormuz to resume normally. Brent crude, the international benchmark, fell 5.8 percent to $103.54 a barrel after climbing above $115 earlier this week. The decline came after US President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz could be “OPEN TO ALL” if Iran agrees to a reported deal, though he did not provide further details.

articles.title: Israeli raids continue across occupied West Bank
articles.summary: Israeli raids continue across occupied West Bank Israeli forces continued military operations across the occupied West Bank on Wednesday, including the detention of a young Palestinian man in the city of Tubas, according to the Wafa news agency. Israeli troops stormed Qalandiya refugee camp north of occupied Jerusalem, deploying across neighbourhoods as snipers took positions on rooftops. In the town of Shuqba, east of Ramallah, Israeli forces demolished a house during raids that also led to the detention of several Palestinians, including a woman and her daughter. articles.title: Trump says ‘very good talks’ held with Iran in past 24 hours
articles.summary: Trump says ‘very good talks’ held with Iran in past 24 hours US President Donald Trump said Washington held “very good talks” with Iran over the past 24 hours as Tehran reviews a US proposal aimed at ending the US-Israeli war on Iran. “It’s very possible that we’ll make a deal,” Trump told reporters at the Oval Office.
